medications.drug_name: simple oral analgesia (e.g. paracetamol) while awaiting ambulance
medications.indication_rationale: May provide some pain relief while awaiting emergency transport, but is secondary to escalation and should not delay it.
contraindications_in_this_case: Avoid oral intake if airway compromise is suspected

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Cool (not ice-cold) running water over the burn for the first 20 minutes if not already done, then keep it loosely covered; sit upright and avoid eating or drinking given possible airway involvement.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Cool water reduces burn depth; upright positioning and nil oral intake are safest given suspected airway injury.

patient_education_points.point: Your facial burn along with your hoarse voice and coughing up sooty material raise concern that you may have breathed in hot gas, which can cause your airway to swell over the next few hours, even though you can still speak normally right now.
Call 000 for an ambulance immediately — this needs emergency assessment, likely including a breathing tube placed early as a precaution before any swelling worsens.
Sit upright, avoid drinking or eating anything, and try to stay as calm and still as possible while waiting.
If your breathing becomes noisy or harsh (stridor), or you feel like you can't get enough air, tell 000 immediately — this means the swelling may be worsening.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency transport to a specialised burns unit
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Airway protection, burns fluid resuscitation, TBSA assessment, and carbon monoxide/cyanide toxicity screening
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Not applicable — already an emergency requiring immediate transport

must_include_items: Explicit statement that inhalation injury is suspected despite current normal speech
Advice to remain upright and avoid oral intake
acceptable_alternatives: Cool running water first aid if not already applied
errors_of_omission: No recognition of possible inhalation injury
errors_of_commission: Recommending ice or ice-cold water on the burn
Reassuring the patient that no emergency care is needed because they can still speak
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Failing to escalate immediately due to reassurance from current normal speech is a critical, potentially fatal safety failure.
